description: Publicly documented patron-client political structures ('godfather networks') in Nigeria. Only documented entries included.

data.description: Tinubu is the dominant political patron of Lagos and the broader Southwest. Controlled Lagos governorship succession from 1999; Fashola and Sanwo-Olu were his protégés. Instrumental in forming APC (2013) and selecting APC presidential nominees.
documented_clients: Babatunde Raji Fashola (former Lagos governor, later minister)
data.current_status: Now president; his network extends nationally through APC

data.description: Former Rivers governor (2015-2023), now FCT Minister. Wike retains significant political networks in Rivers despite being replaced by Fubara (his former ally turned rival). The Wike-Fubara rift (2023-2026) is one of Nigeria's most significant current political conflicts.
documented_clients: Siminalayi Fubara (initially Wike's ally; now rival)
data.current_status: FCT Minister; active patron in Rivers and PDP internal politics

data.description: Saraki dynasty in Kwara State. Father was Olusola Saraki (long-time Kwara powerbroker). Bukola Saraki was governor then Senate President. The dynasty was defeated in 2019 ('OtogeGang' movement). Kwara went to APC under AbdulRazaq.
data.current_status: Significantly weakened after 2019 Kwara elections; no longer dominant in Kwara

sources.name: Wikipedia — 2019 Nigerian Senate election (Saraki lost his seat)

data.description: Kwankwaso-sphere known as 'Kwankwasiyya' movement. Mass following in Kano; NNPP holds Kano governorship through Abba Yusuf (Kwankwaso ally). Significant social welfare/educational patronage network.

gaps.note: Edwin Clark is a major elder statesman/patron in Ijaw/SS politics. Not yet fetched.
SE and SS godfather networks incompletely documented here
